76356.subject: 🔷 The Daily: Crypto ethics language draws Democratic pushback
76356.body:
Pakistan launches crypto investigation unit to crack down on digital asset crimes | HTX keeps rotating wallets to evade UK sanctions screening: TRM Labs | 'You will have a painful fall': SEC's Peirce warns crypto vault builders against twisting securities law | Zilliqa halts native transactions over bug in its Ledger app dating to 2019 |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Jason Shubnell

Happy Wednesday! *Bitcoin's* rebound is unfolding against a historically quiet summer market, with analysts ( https://www.theblock.co/post/409398/optimistic-signals-meet-68000-wall-bitcoin-recovery-faces-next-test-analysts?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) pointing to low institutional positioning and subdued volatility as signs that conviction has yet to return.

In today's newsletter, the *Senate* releases its latest *Clarity Act* text, *Pakistan* launches a crypto investigation unit, *Peirce* warns crypto vault builders, and more.

Meanwhile, *Polymarket* said it was "surprised" that *France* blocked its site as an information source and plans a legal challenge ( https://www.theblock.co/post/409395/polymarket-surprised-france-blocked-site-information-source-plans-legal-challenge?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ).

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*👀 Senate releases latest version of the Clarity Act including software developer protections and an ethics provision with a 'sunset' date in 2029*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/409442/senate-releases-latest-clarity-act-software-developer-protections-ethics-provision-sunset-date-2029?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

*Republicans* released the latest text of the *Clarity Act* , combining the *Senate Agriculture* and *Banking Committee* versions into a 616-page bill that could reach a full Senate vote as soon as next week.

* The text addresses the ethics fight over how to limit presidents, members of Congress, and other federal officials from profiting from digital assets while in office, the final hurdle to passing the legislation.
* An ethics section would block public officials and their spouses from issuing or sponsoring digital assets, though they could still invest in them, with the provision set to sunset at noon on Jan. 20, 2029.
* The *Justice Department* would enforce the ethics rule rather than stat

76355.subject: Citadel Just Wrote a $400M Check
76355.body:
----------
_**Your Weekly Deep Dive into The World of Crypto**_

**July 22, 2026 · ****5 Min Read**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/f25e3d24-935f-4c8a-8333-acd8bb5001e7/image.png?t=1784698271)
Caption: **^ As of July 22, 2026, 2:00 AM ET | Sources: CoinMarketCap, Yahoo Finance, CoinDesk, Alternative.me, SoSoValue^**

## 📡** THIS WEEK IN CRYPTO**

**GM, Readers!**

Rate cut hopes died on Tuesday. US strikes on Iran ran into a ninth straight night by Sunday. And Ken Griffin quietly wrote a[ $400M check](https://www.theblock.co/post/408688/citadel-securities-invests-400-million-crypto-com-20-billion-valuation) for a crypto exchange. Bitcoin finished the week near $64,700, little changed overall, suggesting the market is either bored or holding its breath.

June[ CPI cooled to 3.5%](https://www.bls.gov/cpi/). Fed Chair Warsh took the mic ninety minutes later and killed the vibe. Polymarket priced a July[ Fed Hold at roughly 94%](https://www.tradingview.com/news/newsbtc:d65fb0a1f094b:0-polymarket-fed-hold-odds-hit-94-as-softer-inflation-boosts-bitcoin-mood/). ETH ETFs outpulled BTC ETFs again, an a16z-linked whale started dumping HYPE, and Solana meme traders continued to lose their minds and their money.

_**Let’s get into it...**_


----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/6c53930f-b4d7-4e7d-baee-6a88bb56a6da/image.png?t=1781097813)
Caption: 

----------
# 🐋 THE BIG WHALE MOVES

### **1. Citadel Just Wrote a $400M Check for Crypto.com**

Ken Griffin's Citadel Securities invested $400M in Crypto.com at a $20B valuation on July 16, the exchange's[ first-ever institutional funding round](https://decrypt.co/373693/crypto-com-hits-20b-valuation-after-400m-citadel-securities-investment) in 10 years. The deal mirrors Citadel's[ $200M Kraken investment](https://blog.kraken.com/news/800-million-raise-to-advance-strategic-roadmap) from November 2025 at the same $20B mark.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/4a8ee0e0-bb8d-4c35-b6cb-72234c86bcbd/image.png?t=1784698351)
Follow image link: (https://x.com/kris/status/2077820415376756794?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw%7Ctwcamp%5Etweetembed%7Ctwterm%5E2077820415376756794%7Ctwgr%5E7d0ef27b7b640da2a3d5ee9912b8e515ad79e1d0%7Ctwcon%5Es1_&ref_url=https%3A%2F%2Fdecrypt.co%2F373693%2Fcrypto-com-hits-20b-valuation-after-400m-citadel-securities-investment)
Caption: _**Source:**_[ X Post (@kris)](https://x.com/kris/status/2077820415376756794?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw%7Ctwcamp%5Etweetembed%7Ctwterm%5E2077820415376756794%7Ctwgr%5E7d0ef27b7b640da2a3d5ee9912b8e515ad79e1d0%7Ctwcon%5Es1_&ref_url=https%3A%2F%2Fdecrypt.co%2F373693%2Fcrypto-com-hits-20b-valuation-after-400m-citadel-securities-investment)

This rapid growth marks a significant retail shift in the Layer 2 ecosystem. Read full story 🏛️SEC’s Peirce warns crypto vaults, onchain lending may fall under securities laws SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce cautioned that decentralized lending products and crypto vaults are not automatically exempt from federal oversight. The political friction comes as analysts lower the probability of Bitcoin reaching $200,000 this year. Read full story 💰Zhibao Technology enters non-binding term sheet with Joyer Tech for $4B Bitcoin PIPE financing Zhibao Technology has announced a massive $4 billion Bitcoin PIPE deal with Joyer Tech to bolster its balance sheet. The move follows a Nasdaq deficiency notice, highlighting a high-stakes pivot into digital asset reserves. See details 🛠️Uniswap’s DualPool hook audited and ready for deployment The DualPool hook for Uniswap v4 is now open-source and audited, allowing liquidity providers to earn vault yield on idle assets. This upgrade aims to significantly improve capital efficiency for decentralized exchange participants. Read full story 📉US Treasury 30-year bond yield exceeds 5% for longest streak since 2007 The 30-year Treasury yield’s prolonged stay above 5% is reshaping risk asset dynamics and increasing debt-servicing costs globally. This persistent macroeconomic pressure continues to challenge the valuation of high-growth tech and crypto sectors. See details 🤖AMD to invest up to $5B in Anthropic, challenging Nvidia in AI infrastructure AMD plans a massive $5 billion investm

Brooke Unger

Senior digital editor



 Salaries are high in London. It can be tricky to work out why: because skilled people, who would probably earn a lot anywhere, live there? Or because the city itself lifts wages for all? A group of researchers has disentangled the impact of “place” and “person”. Our tool uses the results to compare location premiums across

76352.subject: Claude vs Kimi and Qwen: Who wins?
76352.body:
The Atlas

Автор: GenAI Works


Автор: GenAI Works

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/claude-vs-kimi-qwen-who-wins-genai-works-hiwnc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BQGEZE5X5Q6ei8rLicULrHA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3ChlcQHBGWUsk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrw9jir1~r1-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrw9jir1-r1
  
Welcome back! This edition is all about what actually makes agents reliable: memory instead of re-reading, context you can measure, two models arguing their way to a better answer and a skill that makes any assistant get to the point.  In today's Atlas Newsletter: Meterless: an ex-Anthropic engineer open-sources a real memory layer for agents. Qwen3.8-Max vs Kimi K3: two Chinese giants on real code, and why running both won. Agents don't fail alone: a Chicago paper that measures the context behind every failure. i-have-adhd: the GitHub skill that stops your coding assistant from burying the answer. PS: Want to collaborate with the biggest GTM engine and distribution moat for AI companies? You can feature your tool with us and reach millions. An ex-Anthropic engineer just open-sourced a context layer for AI agents It's called Meterless, and it's one of the more ambitious attempts to give AI agents real memory. Most agents today are trapped in a loop.  You give them a task. They complete one step. Then they reread the conversation, reconstruct what happened and reason through the same information again. Every single step. That creates three problems:  → Token costs keep growing → Context windows eventually fill up → The agent still doesn't truly understand your world Meterless takes a different route.  Instead of one enormous prompt, it gives agents several specialized systems:  → H-MEM builds durable, hierarchical memory → The World Model tracks users, tasks, events, documents and relationships → The Markovian Engine breaks long tasks into bounded steps and carries only what the next action needs → Scout detects intent, ambiguity, risk and the right tools before execution begins The compounding effect is the interesting part.  In the project's example, the same 12-step task took 12 chunks without memory and 8 with accumulated memory, for an estimated 814 tokens saved.  A modeled 20-step workflow used 86% fewer input tokens than continuously appending the full history. At 100 steps, the estimate reaches 97%. These figures are modeled rather than independent benchmarks, so treat them as the vendor's for now.  The underlying idea still holds: traditional agent context grows with every step, while Meterless keeps what passes between steps bounded. An agent that forgets everything isn't really an agent. It's a chatbot starting over on repeat. So are you building agent
